title: Doing Meta-Analysis in R
description: An accessible hands-on guide to conducting meta-analyses in R, covering effect sizes, pooling, heterogeneity, forest plots, subgroup analyses, meta-regression, and publication bias. Also includes advanced methods such as multilevel, network, Bayesian, and SEM meta-analysis, alongside practical tools for power analysis, risk of bias plots, and reporting. Requires no expert-level programming or statistical background.
subject: general
